questions.q: Whose son was King Janamejaya?
options: Abhimanyu
Yudhishthira
Parikshit
Vajra
questions.explanation: Janamejaya was the son of Parikshit and succeeded him on the throne of Hastinapura.

questions.q: What great rite did Janamejaya undertake as king?
options: The sarpa satra, a snake sacrifice
The ashvamedha, a horse sacrifice
The rajasuya, an imperial sacrifice
The vajapeya, a soma sacrifice
questions.explanation: Janamejaya launched the sarpa satra, a great sacrificial rite intended to destroy the serpent race.

questions.q: Why did Janamejaya hold the snake sacrifice?
options: To win a boon of immortality from the gods
To avenge his father, who died of Takshaka's bite
To cleanse the lingering sin of the Kurukshetra war
To drive the serpents out of his new capital
questions.explanation: His father Parikshit had been killed by the serpent Takshaka, and the sacrifice was Janamejaya's vengeance.

questions.q: Which serpent king was the chief target of Janamejaya's sacrifice?
options: Vasuki
Shesha
Karkotaka
Takshaka
questions.explanation: Takshaka, whose bite had killed Parikshit, was the serpent the fire was meant above all to consume.

questions.q: Who halted the snake sacrifice at the last moment?
options: The boy sage Astika
The sage Uttanka
The serpent king Vasuki
The priest Vaishampayana
questions.explanation: The young sage Astika, half-brahmin and half-naga by birth, won a boon from the king and asked that the rite cease.

questions.q: Who recited the Mahabharata to Janamejaya during the sacrifice?
options: Ugrashravas the bard
Vyasa himself
Vaishampayana, Vyasa's disciple
Sanjaya the far-seeing
questions.explanation: At Vyasa's bidding, his disciple Vaishampayana recited the whole epic to the king between the rites.

questions.q: Whose composition was the great epic that Janamejaya heard at his sacrifice?
options: Valmiki's
Narada's
Shuka's
Vyasa's
questions.explanation: The epic recited at the snake sacrifice was the Mahabharata composed by Vyasa, who sat present in the assembly.

questions.q: What happened to the serpents as the sacrifice proceeded?
options: They fled the earth for the netherworld
Called by name, they fell into the sacrificial fire
They turned to stone around the altar
They shed their venom and became harmless
questions.explanation: As the priests chanted, serpents of every kind were drawn helplessly by name into the blazing fire.

questions.q: Where did Takshaka hide from the summons of the sacrificial mantras?
options: In the depths of the ocean
In the netherworld of the nagas
Under the protection of Indra in heaven
Inside a fruit in the king's palace
questions.explanation: Terrified, Takshaka fled to Indra, who sheltered the serpent in his own abode.

questions.q: Janamejaya was the great-grandson of which Pandava hero?
options: Arjuna
Bhima
Yudhishthira
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: The line ran from Arjuna to Abhimanyu to Parikshit to Janamejaya.

questions.q: Which sage spurred Janamejaya to undertake the snake sacrifice?
options: Shamika
Astika
Jaratkaru
Uttanka
questions.explanation: The sage Uttanka, nursing his own grudge against Takshaka, urged the king to avenge his father with the sarpa satra.

questions.q: How exactly did Astika bring the sacrifice to an end?
options: He smothered the sacred fire with offerings of milk
Offered any boon for his praise of the rite, he asked that it cease
He proved before the priests that the snakes were innocent
He cursed the fire so it could consume no more serpents
questions.explanation: Janamejaya, delighted by the boy's praises, offered him any boon, and Astika asked that the sacrifice stop, a word the king could not retract.

questions.q: Who were the parents of Astika, the boy who stopped the sacrifice?
options: The sage Jaratkaru and Vasuki's serpent sister
The sage Shamika and a naga princess
The serpent Takshaka and a mortal queen
The sage Kashyapa and the goddess Kadru
questions.explanation: Astika was born to the ascetic Jaratkaru and the serpent maiden given to him by her brother Vasuki.

questions.q: Who sent young Astika to save the serpent race from the fire?
options: His father, the ascetic Jaratkaru
The creator god Brahma
His uncle Vasuki, king of the serpents
The grieving serpent mother Kadru
questions.explanation: As the fire consumed his kin, the desperate Vasuki sent his sister's son Astika to halt the rite.

questions.q: What ancient curse doomed the serpents to burn in Janamejaya's fire?
options: Astika's grandfather cursed them for biting his kin
Their own mother Kadru cursed them for disobeying her
Brahma cursed them for poisoning the young earth
Parikshit cursed them with his dying breath
questions.explanation: Long before, Kadru had cursed her disobedient sons to perish in a king's snake sacrifice, and Janamejaya's rite fulfilled it.

questions.q: What had Kadru demanded that her disobedient serpent sons refused to do?
options: Carry her across the ocean to see the divine horse
Bite the horse Uchchaihshravas and kill it
Steal the nectar of immortality for her
Turn the tail of the white horse Uchchaihshravas black for her wager
questions.explanation: To win her wager with Vinata, Kadru ordered her sons to darken the celestial horse's tail, and cursed those who refused.

questions.q: What was Takshaka's plight at the very instant the sacrifice was stopped?
options: He hung suspended in mid-air, about to drop into the flames
He lay coiled in hiding at the bottom of the fire pit
He was halfway out of Indra's grasp in heaven
He stood disguised in human form among the priests
questions.explanation: Torn from Indra's protection, Takshaka was falling toward the fire and hung frozen in mid-air when the rite was halted.

questions.q: How did the priests dislodge Takshaka from Indra's protection?
options: They cursed Indra's throne to burn until he yielded
They summoned Garuda to drag the serpent down
They poured offerings naming Takshaka together with Indra himself
They sent Astika to plead before heaven's court
questions.explanation: When the libations invoked Takshaka along with Indra, the alarmed god cast the serpent off rather than be drawn in himself.

questions.q: Which of these were Janamejaya's brothers?
options: Yaudheya, Devaka, and Sarvaga
Prativindhya, Shrutasoma, and Shatanika
Chitrasena, Sushena, and Satyasena
Shrutasena, Ugrasena, and Bhimasena
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva names Shrutasena, Ugrasena, and Bhimasena as the brothers who sat with Janamejaya at his rites.

questions.q: Who was Janamejaya's mother?
options: Uttara
Madravati
Vapushtama
Subhadra
questions.explanation: Janamejaya was born to Parikshit's queen Madravati.

questions.q: What grudge did the sage Uttanka bear against the serpent Takshaka?
options: Takshaka had bitten his guru to death
Takshaka had burned down his hermitage
Takshaka had stolen the earrings he was carrying for his guru's wife
Takshaka had poisoned the well of his ashram
questions.explanation: Takshaka had snatched the queen's earrings that Uttanka was bringing as his teacher's fee, forcing him to chase the serpent to the naga world.

questions.q: What prophecy hung over the snake sacrifice from the time its grounds were built?
options: That a brahmin would cause it to be left incomplete
That its fire would never afterward be extinguished
That its smoke would blind the king who held it
That it would one day consume the king's own line
questions.explanation: A builder versed in omens declared at the laying of the platform that a brahmin would interrupt the rite, as Astika did.

questions.q: Who later retold the epic, as heard at Janamejaya's sacrifice, to the sages of the Naimisha forest?
options: Vaishampayana, repeating his recital
Ugrashravas, the bard son of Lomaharshana
Astika, in gratitude for the serpents
Shuka, the son of Vyasa
questions.explanation: The bard Ugrashravas carried the tale from the snake sacrifice to Shaunaka's assembly at Naimisha, the epic's outermost frame.

questions.q: What lasting protection for people is linked to Astika's deed at the sacrifice?
options: Snakes may never again enter a sacrificial hall
Serpents lost their venom against all kings
No serpent may bite a sleeping brahmin
Those who recall Astika's story are safe from snakes
questions.explanation: The grateful serpents granted that whoever remembers Astika and his deed need have no fear of snakes.

questions.q: Which city did Janamejaya conquer around the time of his snake sacrifice?
options: Takshashila
Mathura
Girivraja
Kampilya
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva notes that Janamejaya had brought Takshashila under his sway before the long rite.

questions.q: Who was Janamejaya's queen?
options: Madravati
Sudeshna
Devika
Vapushtama
questions.explanation: Janamejaya married Vapushtama, a princess of Kashi; Madravati was his mother's name.

questions.q: What was the name of Astika's serpent mother?
options: Manasa
Kadru
Jaratkaru
Ulupi
questions.explanation: She bore the same name, Jaratkaru, as the sage she married; later tradition identifies her with the snake goddess Manasa.

questions.q: Why did Vasuki give his sister in marriage to the ascetic Jaratkaru?
options: To win the sage's protection against Garuda
So her son, as Brahma foretold, would one day save the serpents
To end the ancient feud between sages and snakes
To earn the merit that would lift Kadru's curse from himself
questions.explanation: Brahma had foretold that a son of Jaratkaru and Vasuki's sister would halt the snake sacrifice, so Vasuki long kept her ready for the sage.

questions.q: What question of Janamejaya's launched the full recitation of the epic at his sacrifice?
options: Why his ancestors, the Kurus and Pandavas, had slaughtered one another
How his father Parikshit had sinned against the sage
Whether a king may rightly avenge blood with fire
How Krishna could have allowed the great war to happen
questions.explanation: Janamejaya asked how the breach arose that drove his ancestors to mutual destruction, and Vaishampayana answered with the whole epic.

questions.q: Vyasa himself attended the sacrifice; what did he do when the king begged him for the tale?
options: He recited it himself over the course of three years
He directed his disciple Vaishampayana to recite it
He handed the king a written scroll of the verses
He sent for Ganesha, who had penned the epic
questions.explanation: Vyasa turned to his pupil Vaishampayana and bade him narrate the epic as he had learned it from his master.
